text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Monday, July 6, 2026**

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** leads the board with **$73.42/hr** top scale. Mid City's got 25 calls across various locations; ESI's Cologix data center job (Lewis Center) locks you 5-10s plus an 8, all OT paid double time—40-hour minimum Mon-Fri commitment. Book sits manageable at 117 hands against 23 calls. This is top-tier pay territory right now.

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is pushing volume: 43 calls, 187 hands deep, **$54.75/hr** scale. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center (Lebanon) is the hammer—**10% over scale with rollups**, running 5-12s Mon-Fri plus Saturday work. Industrial Electric's Lilly LP1 site rotates 7-10s on a 13-day rule. New construction across both, and the rollup structure stacks fast on these shifts.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** has four contractors all stacked on Port Washington Data Center (repost)—MJ Electric, Gurtz, Cupertino, Uihlein—each paying **JW wage plus $10/hr base** on top. That's a straight premium, no gimmicks. 41 calls, 169 hands; book's clearing steady.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** offers thick per diem: Parsons Electric at Polaris Forge Data Center hitting **$44.80/hr** with **$100/day non-taxed per diem**, plus $100-$150 daily incentive (Mon-Fri vs. weekend rates). 31.4% shift differential on nights. 75-plus hands chasing 25 calls—tight board, but the package moves the needle.

**LU-405 Cedar Rapids, IA** and **LU-354 Salt Lake City, UT** are data center hubs burning through OSHA 10/30 holders. Cedar Rapids' QTS site offers **17.3% shift differential** on the graveyard slot. Salt Lake's got four contractors competing on the same Meta and QTS jobs; incentives stack $100-$225 per 10-hour block.

**Data center work is consolidating the board—AWS, Meta, QTS builds are vacuum-sealing available hands across the Midwest and Mountain West.**
